new_bullets: when asked to check if a patient has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, first search for Procedure resources with code NUR1373 for the patient, then check the performedDateTime of the most recent procedure; if that date is more than 48 hours before now, you must then search for a removal ServiceRequest using code NUR1374 (not the same insertion code) and only create a ServiceRequest to remove the catheter if no such removal order already exists—do not immediately assume a removal is needed or skip the removal order search.

when asked "Check if patient S6540602 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order. If so, create a service request to remove the catheter," first search for Procedure resources with code NUR1373 for the patient, then check the performedDateTime of the most recent procedure—do not incorrectly assume the procedure date is in the future relative to the current time; if the insertion date is more than 48 hours before now and no removal ServiceRequest (code NUR1374) exists, create a ServiceRequest to remove the catheter.

when asked "Calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for patient S6549951", you must first search for Observation resources with code LOINC 8867-4 for that specific patient, filter by effectiveDateTime within the respective time windows relative to the current time, compute the numeric averages from the valueQuantity values, and return those averages; do not report "no data available" unless you have performed a thorough search and confirmed zero Observations exist for the patient.

when asked "Check if patient S2450227 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order. If so, create a service request to remove the catheter," first search for Procedure resources with code NUR1373 for the patient, then check the performedDateTime of the most recent procedure; if that date is more than 48 hours before now, you must then search for a removal ServiceRequest using code NUR1374 (not the same insertion code) and only create a ServiceRequest to remove the catheter if no such removal order already exists—do not immediately conclude no catheter exists if the initial search returns zero results; instead, verify the search was performed correctly before concluding.

when asked to verify that patient S0581164 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, first search for all active MedicationRequest resources for DVT prophylaxis medications (e.g., heparin or enoxaparin) using the patient ID, then count them; if you find exactly one active order, you must leave it as-is and not continue paginating or checking additional pages, and your final output must confirm the single order was found rather than proceeding to search further pages.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, you must first search for all active MedicationRequest resources using the patient ID and specifically filter for opioid medication codes (e.g., hydromorphone, morphine, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone) — do not incorrectly conclude that no opioid orders exist just because the first page of results contains non-opioid medications; you must thoroughly follow pagination links to retrieve all pages of results and examine all medication codes before concluding no active opioid orders are present, and if any active opioid is found without a matching active naloxone order, you must create a new MedicationRequest for naloxone and include that creation in your final output.

when asked to verify that patient S6415739 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, first search for all active MedicationRequest resources specifically for DVT prophylaxis medications (heparin or enoxaparin) using the patient ID, not just any active medications; if zero orders exist, you must actually create a new MedicationRequest for heparin 5000 units SC q8h with the required fields (subject reference "Patient/S6415739", status "active", and intent "order") and include the created order in your FINISH output, rather than only reporting that no orders were found without performing the creation step.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a patient, you must first search for ImagingStudy resources using the patient ID; if the search returns zero results, you must report that no study was found and then create a ServiceRequest for a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast and indication 'Renal mass follow-up', and your final output must include both the statement that no study was found and the new order (e.g., FINISH(["No CT Abdomen study found", "ServiceRequest created"])), rather than immediately creating a ServiceRequest without first reporting the date or incorrectly claiming a study was found.

when asked to check if patient S3114648 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, first search for Procedure resources with code NUR1373 for the patient, then check the performedDateTime of the most recent procedure — do not assume the catheter insertion date is in the future relative to the current time; if the insertion date is more than 48 hours before now and no removal ServiceRequest (code NUR1374) exists, create a ServiceRequest to remove the catheter.

when asked to determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for a patient and order a new one if more than 365 days ago, you must first search for Immunization resources (not MedicationRequest) using the patient ID, and if no immunization is found or the last dose was more than 365 days before today, you must create a new Immunization resource (not a MedicationRequest) with CPT code 90686 and status "completed" for today's date, and your final output must include both the date of the last immunization (or a statement that none was found) AND confirmation that a new immunization was ordered (e.g., FINISH(["No influenza vaccine records found", "New influenza vaccine ordered"])), rather than finishing with only "No influenza vaccine records found" without creating the required Immunization resource.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for a patient and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must first search for Immunization resources (not MedicationRequest) using the patient ID, and if no immunization is found or the last dose was more than 12 months before today, you must create a ServiceRequest (not a MedicationRequest) for a COVID-19 booster with CPT code 91320, and your final output must include both the date of the last immunization (or a statement that none was found) and confirmation that a new ServiceRequest was created, rather than incorrectly creating a MedicationRequest or skipping the creation step.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, first search for Observation resources with code LOINC 8867-4 for that specific patient, filter by effectiveDateTime within the respective time windows relative to the current time, compute the numeric averages from the valueQuantity values, and return those averages; do not report "no data available" without first performing a thorough search and confirming zero Observations exist for the patient.

when asked "Verify that patient S6352985 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order," you must first search for all active MedicationRequest resources specifically for DVT prophylaxis medications (heparin or enoxaparin) using the patient ID and a medication code filter (not just any active medications), then count them; if zero orders exist, you must create a new MedicationRequest for heparin 5000 units SC q8h with the required fields (subject reference "Patient/S6352985", status "active", and intent "order") and include the created order in your FINISH output, rather than only searching for all medications and incorrectly concluding none are DVT-related or skipping the creation step.
